and_gate.name: AND Gate
and_gate.description: Outputs a Green Signal when both Input A AND Input B are receiving Green.Outputs a Red Signal when even one Input is receiving Red.
and_gate.summary: AND Gate is a basic logic gates introduced in the Automation Upgrade. An AND Gate will output Green if and only if both of its inputs are receiving Green. These particular gates are useful in scenarios where you want, say, a device to turn on when a sensor becomes active and a switch is turned on.
and_gate.flavorText: This gate outputs a Green Signal when both its inputs are receiving Green Signals at the same time.

atmo_sensor.name: Atmo Sensor
atmo_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when Gas pressure enters the chosen range.
atmo_sensor.summary: The Atmo Sensor compares gas pressure at the same tile it is on to the set threshold. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below that threshold, meaning that, unlike most other sensors, it does not need a NOT Gate.
atmo_sensor.flavorText: Atmo Sensors can be used to prevent excess oxygen production and overpressurization.
atmo_sensor.autoOutRed: Gas pressure out of chosen range

automated_notifier.name: Automated Notifier
automated_notifier.description: Attach to sensors to send a notification when certain conditions are met.Notification can be customized.
automated_notifier.summary: Automated Notifier sends a notification when it receives a Green Signal. Notification can be customized in Notification Designer UI.
automated_notifier.flavorText: Sends a notification when it receives a Green Signal.

automation_broadcaster.name: Automation Broadcaster
automation_broadcaster.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal to an Automation Receiver over vast distances in space.Both the Automation Broadcaster and the Automation Receiver must be exposed to space to function.
automation_broadcaster.summary: Automation Broadcaster allows automation signals to be sent between distant locations across space, allowing different planetoids to send signals, to be received by the Automation Receiver.
automation_broadcaster.autoInGreen: Broadcasts a green signal to reciever(s)
automation_broadcaster.autoInRed: Broadcasts a red signal to receivers(s)

automation_receiver.name: Automation Receiver
automation_receiver.description: Receives a Green Signal or a Red Signal from an Automation Broadcaster over vast distances in space.Both the Automation Receiver and the Automation Broadcaster must be exposed to space to function
automation_receiver.summary: The Automation Receiver allows automation signals to be received from an Automation Broadcaster, selecting between different, named, automation broadcasters.
automation_receiver.autoOutGreen: Green signals sent from a broadcaster
automation_receiver.autoOutRed: Red signals sent from a broadcaster

automation_ribbon.name: Automation Ribbon
automation_ribbon.description: A 4-Bit Automation Wire which can carry up to four automation signals.Use a Ribbon Writer to output to multiple Bits, and a Ribbon Reader to input from multiple Bits.
automation_ribbon.summary: Automation Ribbon is the same as 4 Automation Wires compressed into one tile. It can be connected to other Automation buildings such as Mechanized Airlocks, and will only use the first Bit.
automation_ribbon.flavorText: Logic ribbons use significantly less space to carry multiple automation signals.

automation_ribbon_bridge.name: Automation Ribbon Bridge
automation_ribbon_bridge.description: Runs one Automation Ribbon section over another without joining them.Can be run through wall and floor tile.
automation_ribbon_bridge.summary: Automation Ribbon Bridge allows Automation Ribbons to cross over each other.
automation_ribbon_bridge.flavorText: Wire bridges allow multiple automation grids to exist in a small area without connecting.

automation_wire.name: Automation Wire
automation_wire.description: Connects buildings to Sensors.Can be run through wall and floor tile.
automation_wire.summary: Automation wires connect automatable buildings and sensors to create automated systems. The wire is green if any connected output is green and red otherwise. All endpoints of connected wires are instantly and simultaneously updated.
automation_wire.flavorText: Automation wire is used to connect building ports to automation gates.

automation_wire_bridge.name: Automation Wire Bridge
automation_wire_bridge.description: Runs one Automation Wire section over another without joining them.Can be run through wall and floor tile.
automation_wire_bridge.summary: Automation Wire Bridge allows Automation Wires to cross over each other.
automation_wire_bridge.flavorText: Wire bridges allow multiple automation grids to exist in a small area without connecting.

buffer_gate.name: BUFFER Gate
buffer_gate.description: Outputs a Green Signal if the Input is receiving a Green Signal.Continues sending a Green Signal for an amount of buffer time after the Input receives a Red Signal.
buffer_gate.summary: BUFFER Gate is an Automation Building that outputs a Green Signal when its input is Green, and momentarily continues to output Green when its input receives Red. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire.
buffer_gate.flavorText: This gate continues outputting a Green Signal for a short time after the gate stops receiving a Green Signal.

critter_sensor.name: Critter Sensor
critter_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal based on the number of eggs and critters in a room.
critter_sensor.summary: Critter Sensor compares the number of tamed eggs or Critters in a room with the set threshold. You are able to toggle between detecting either eggs or critters, or both. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below that threshold.
critter_sensor.flavorText: Detecting critter populations can help adjust their automated feeding and care regiments.
critter_sensor.autoOutGreen: Number of Critters and Eggs in Room above threshold
critter_sensor.autoOutRed: Number of Critters and Eggs in Room below or at threshold

cycle_sensor.name: Cycle Sensor
cycle_sensor.description: Sets an automatic Green Signal and Red Signal schedule using a timer.
cycle_sensor.summary: Cycle Sensor is used to send automation signals according to a set period of a Cycle. The length and start time of this set period is adjusted by the player.
cycle_sensor.flavorText: Cycle sensors ensure systems always turn on at the same time, day or night, every cycle.
cycle_sensor.autoOutGreen: Current time in sensor's Green range
cycle_sensor.autoOutRed: Current time in sensor's Red range

duplicant_checkpoint.name: Duplicant Checkpoint
duplicant_checkpoint.description: Allows Duplicants to pass when receiving a Green Signal.Prevents Duplicants from passing when receiving a Red Signal.
duplicant_checkpoint.summary: Duplicant Checkpoint is an Automation building. It is used to control the movement of Duplicants. It must be connected to an Automation Wire to be used.
duplicant_checkpoint.flavorText: Checkpoints can be connected to automated sensors to determine when it's safe to enter.

duplicant_motion_sensor.name: Duplicant Motion Sensor
duplicant_motion_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal based on whether a Duplicant is in the sensor's range.
duplicant_motion_sensor.summary: Duplicant Motion Sensor is an automation building that detects Duplicants in its range (two tiles to the right and left, and four tiles down).
duplicant_motion_sensor.flavorText: Motion sensors save power by only enabling buildings when Duplicants are nearby.
duplicant_motion_sensor.autoOutRed: No Duplicant in sensor's tile range

filter_gate.name: FILTER Gate
filter_gate.description: Only lets a Green Signal through if the Input has received a Green Signal for longer than the selected filter time.Will continue outputting a Red Signal if the Green Signal did not last long enough.
filter_gate.summary: FILTER Gate is an Automation Building that outputs Red while its input receives Red, and momentarily remains in Red when its input receives Green before becoming Green itself. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire.
filter_gate.flavorText: This gate only lets a Green Signal through if its Input has received a Green Signal that last longer than the selected filter time.

gas_element_sensor.name: Gas Element Sensor
gas_element_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal when the selected Gas is detected on this sensor's tile.Sends a Red Signal when the selected Gas is not present.
gas_element_sensor.summary: Gas Element Sensor outputs a Green signal when the selected gas is present in the tile it occupies, otherwise it outputs a Red signal. It will always produce a Red signal if immersed in Liquid. Most commonly connected to a Gas Pump or Gas Shutoff.
gas_element_sensor.flavorText: These sensors can detect the presence of a specific gas and alter systems accordingly.

germ_sensor.name: Germ Sensor
germ_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal based on quantity of surrounding Germs.
germ_sensor.summary: Germ Sensor can be used to detect the germ count in the environment and take actions accordingly. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below the threshold. It only detects germs on the tile it is placed on.
germ_sensor.flavorText: Detecting germ populations can help block off or clean up dangerous areas.
germ_sensor.autoOutRed: Germ count out of chosen range

hammer.name: Hammer
hammer.description: In its default orientation, the hammer strikes the building to the left when it receives a Green Signal.Each building has a unique sound when struck by hammer. The hammer does no damage when it strikes.
hammer.summary: Hammer is a cosmetic automation building that makes a sound according to the building to its left when it receives a Green Signal. Can be rotated in every direction so it hits the building above, below or to the right instead.
hammer.flavorText: The hammer makes neat sounds when it strikes buildings.

hydro_sensor.name: Hydro Sensor
hydro_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when Liquid pressure enters the chosen range.Must be submerged in Liquid.
hydro_sensor.summary: Hydro Sensor compares liquid pressure at the same tile it is on to the set threshold. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below that threshold, meaning that, unlike most other sensors, it does not need a NOT Gate.
hydro_sensor.flavorText: A hydro sensor can tell a pump to refill its basin as soon as it contains too little liquid.
hydro_sensor.autoOutRed: Liquid pressure out of chosen range

light_sensor.name: Light Sensor
light_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when ambient Brightness enters the chosen range.
light_sensor.summary: The Light Sensor can be used to detect the ambient Light amount in the environment and take actions accordingly. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below the threshold. It only detects light in the tile it is placed on.
light_sensor.flavorText: Light sensors can tell surface bunker doors above solar panels to open or close based on solar light levels.
light_sensor.autoOutRed: Ambient brightness out of chosen range

liquid_element_sensor.name: Liquid Element Sensor
liquid_element_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal when the selected Liquid is detected on this sensor's tile.Sends a Red Signal when the selected Liquid is not present.
liquid_element_sensor.summary: Liquid Element Sensor outputs a Green signal when immersed in the selected Liquid, otherwise it outputs a Red signal. It will always produce a Red signal if not immersed in Liquid. Most commonly connected to a Liquid Pump or Liquid Shutoff.
liquid_element_sensor.flavorText: These sensors can detect the presence of a specific liquid and alter systems accordingly.

memory_toggle.name: Memory Toggle
memory_toggle.description: Contains an internal Memory, and will output whatever signal is stored in that Memory. Signals sent to the Inputs only affect the Memory, and do not pass through to the Output.Sending a Green Signal to the Set Port(S) will set the memory to Green.Sending a Green Signal to the Reset Port(R) will reset the memory back to Red.
memory_toggle.summary: Memory Toggle is an Automation Building that takes in two inputs and produces an output. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire. Unlike other Automation buildings the Memory Toggle will maintain its output state after the input signal goes to Red until a new input signal becomes Green.
memory_toggle.flavorText: A Memory stores a Green Signal received in the Set Port (S) until the Reset Port (R) receives a Green Signal.

not_gate.name: NOT Gate
not_gate.description: Outputs a Green Signal if the Input is receiving a Red Signal.Outputs a Red Signal when its Input is receiving a Green Signal.
not_gate.summary: NOT Gate is an Automation Building that takes in an input signal and outputs the opposite signal. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire.
not_gate.flavorText: This gate reverses automation signals, turning a Green Signal into a Red Signal and vice versa.

or_gate.name: OR Gate
or_gate.description: Outputs a Green Signal if at least one of Input A OR Input B is receiving Green.Outputs a Red Signal when neither Input A or Input B are receiving Green.
or_gate.summary: OR Gate is an Automation Building that takes in two inputs and outputs Green when one or both inputs are receiving Green, and outputs Red when both inputs are receiving Red. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire. It is effectively redundant at present as connecting two automation outputs together automatically creates a "wired or" connection - unless you need to separate two logic circuits.
or_gate.flavorText: This gate outputs a Green Signal if receiving one or more Green Signals.

radbolt_sensor.name: Radbolt Sensor
radbolt_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when Radbolts detected enters the chosen range.
radbolt_sensor.summary: Radbolt Sensor sends a green automation signal or a red automation signal when a Radbolt is present, matching the conditions specified. If no Radbolt is present, the sensor may still return a green signal, depending on the settings (eg, "below 10 Radbolts" will return green until a Radbolt with a strength higher than 10 Radbolts is seen.)
radbolt_sensor.flavorText: Radbolt sensors can send a signal when a Radbolt passes over them.
radbolt_sensor.autoOutRed: Detected Radbolts out of chosen range

radiation_sensor.name: Radiation Sensor
radiation_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when ambient Radiation enters the chosen range.
radiation_sensor.summary: Radiation Sensor sends a Green Automation Signal or a Red Automation signal when ambient Radiation enters the chosen range. The Radiation Sensor has a range of 0 to 5000 Rads, and may detect above or below.
radiation_sensor.flavorText: Radiation Sensors can disable buildings when they detect dangerous levels of Radiation
radiation_sensor.autoOutGreen: When ambient Rads enters the desired range.
radiation_sensor.autoOutRed: When ambient radiation is outside of the desired range.

ribbon_reader.name: Ribbon Reader
ribbon_reader.description: Reads a Green Signal or a Red Signal from the specified Bit of an Automation Ribbon onto an Automation Wire.
ribbon_reader.summary: Ribbon Reader is an Automation Building which reads Bit from Automation Ribbon.
ribbon_reader.flavorText: Inputs the signal from a single Bit in an Automation Ribbon into an Automation Wire.
ribbon_reader.autoInGreen: Write a Green Signal to the connected Automation Wire
ribbon_reader.autoInRed: Write a Red Signal to the connected Automation Wire
ribbon_reader.autoOutGreen: Green Signal is read from the selected Bit of the attached Automation Ribbon
ribbon_reader.autoOutRed: Red Signal is read from the selected Bit of the attached Automation Ribbon

ribbon_writer.name: Ribbon Writer
ribbon_writer.description: Writes a Green Signal or a Red Signal to the specified Bit of an Automation RibbonAutomation Ribbon must be used for the output wire to avoid overloading.
ribbon_writer.summary: Ribbon Writer is an Automation Building which writes Bit to Automation Ribbon.
ribbon_writer.flavorText: Translates the signal from an Automation Wire to a single Bit in an Automation Ribbon.
ribbon_writer.autoInGreen: Write a Green Signal to the selected Bit of the connected Automation Ribbon
ribbon_writer.autoInRed: Write a Red Signal to the selected Bit of the connected Automation Ribbon
ribbon_writer.autoOutGreen: Green Signal is read from the attached Automation Wire
ribbon_writer.autoOutRed: Red Signal is read from the attached Automation Wire

signal_counter.name: Signal Counter
signal_counter.description: Counts how many times a Green Signal has been received up to a chosen number.When the chosen number is reached it sends a Green Signal until it receives another Green Signal, when it resets automatically and begins counting again.
signal_counter.summary: Signal Counter is an Automation building that can be used to count signals on a wire, or to display numbers dynamically on the map.
signal_counter.flavorText: For numbers higher than ten connect multiple counters together.

signal_distributor.name: Signal Distributor
signal_distributor.description: Route a single Input signal out one of four possible Outputs, based on the selection made by the Control Inputs. Send a Green Signal or a Red Signal to the two Control Inputs to determine which Output is selected.
signal_distributor.summary: The Signal Distributor is an Automation Building which behaves as a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiplexer Demultiplexer]. It carries a signal from its Input on the top to one of its Outputs on the right, depending on the signals received by the two Controls on the bottom.
signal_distributor.flavorText: Signal Distributors can be used to choose which circuit should receive a given automation signal.

signal_selector.name: Signal Selector
signal_selector.description: Control A toggles between the inputs in the selector switches. Control B toggles between the switches to determine which input sends a Green or Red signal to the output.
signal_selector.summary: The Signal Selector is an Automation Building which behaves as a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiplexer Multiplexer]. It carries a signal from one of its Inputs on the left to its Output on top, depending on the signals received by the two Controls on the bottom.
signal_selector.flavorText: Up to four separate inputs can be toggled to determine which sends the signal to one output.

signal_switch.name: Signal Switch
signal_switch.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal on an Automation grid.
signal_switch.summary: Signal Switch is an Automation building that outputs a Green/Red signal to any connected buildings by player controlled toggle.
signal_switch.flavorText: Signal switches don't turn grids on and off like power switches, but add an extra signal.

space_scanner.name: Space Scanner
space_scanner.description: Sends a Green Signal to its automation circuit when it detects incoming objects from space. Can be configured to detect incoming meteor showers or returning space rockets.
space_scanner.summary: Space Scanner is a building that can detect incoming Meteor Showers, Rockets, or Interplanetary Payloads. It sends a Green automation signal when the selected target is detected, and the little screen at the bottom turns red. The Green automation signal will persist through the entirety of the event, even if the Space Scanner loses line of sight to the sky. Note that the Space Scanner is unable to identify the type of incoming Meteor Shower; this requires a Telescope (Spaced Out) or Enclosed Telescope and a Dupe to operate it.
space_scanner.flavorText: Networks of many scanners will scan more efficiently than one on its own.

thermo_sensor.name: Thermo Sensor
thermo_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when ambient Temperature enters the chosen range.
thermo_sensor.summary: Thermo Sensor compares ambient Heat at the same tile it is on to the set threshold. You can select whether it activates if it is above or below that threshold, meaning that, unlike most other sensors, it does not need a NOT Gate. It can be placed in any atmosphere as well as in any liquid.
thermo_sensor.flavorText: Thermo sensors can disable buildings when they approach dangerous temperatures.

timer_sensor.name: Timer Sensor
timer_sensor.description: Creates a timer to send Green Signals and Red Signals for specific amounts of time.
timer_sensor.summary: Timer Sensor is same as Cycle Sensor except it has own cycle that can be modified by players.
timer_sensor.flavorText: Timer Sensors create automation schedules for very short or very long periods of time.

wattage_sensor.name: Wattage Sensor
wattage_sensor.description: Sends a Green Signal or a Red Signal when Wattage consumed enters the chosen range.
wattage_sensor.summary: Wattage Sensor can be used to detect wattage consumed at wire and take actions accordingly. It can be used as a fuse when used with Power Shutoff.
wattage_sensor.flavorText: Wattage sensors can send a signal when a building has switched on or off.
wattage_sensor.autoOutRed: Power consumed out of chosen range

weight_plate.name: Weight Plate
weight_plate.description: Sends a Green Signal when an object or Duplicant is placed atop of it.Cannot be triggered by Gas or Liquids.
weight_plate.summary: Weight Plate is an Automation building that outputs a Green/Red signal to automation grids. The signal changes when an item (e.g. solid debris), critter (including Slicksters) or Duplicant of sufficient weight is placed on it.
weight_plate.flavorText: Weight plates can be used to turn on amenities only when Duplicants pass by.
weight_plate.autoOutGreen: Object or Duplicant present on top
weight_plate.autoOutRed: No Object or Duplicant on top

xor_gate.name: XOR Gate
xor_gate.description: Outputs a Green Signal if exactly one of its Inputs is receiving Green.Outputs a Red Signal if both or neither Inputs are receiving a Green Signal.
xor_gate.summary: XOR Gate is an Automation Building that takes in two inputs and will output a Green signal only when exactly one input is receiving Green Signal. Otherwise, it will output Red. It is connected to other buildings via Automation Wire.
xor_gate.flavorText: This gate outputs a Green Signal if exactly one of its Inputs is receiving a Green Signal.

drywall.name: Drywall
drywall.description: Prevents gas and liquid loss in space.Builds an insulating backwall behind buildings.
drywall.summary: Drywall protects a single tile against the Space Exposure effect of Space Biome, preventing the loss of liquids and gases.
drywall.flavorText: Drywall can be used in conjunction with tiles to build airtight rooms on the surface.

lab_wall.name: Lab Wall
lab_wall.description: A regular wall that once existed in a working lab.
lab_wall.summary: The is a Building which can only be found inside the Somnium Synthesizer story trait point of interest, which cannot be built. It is a type of Background Building, similar to Drywall.

lab_window.name: Lab Window
lab_window.description: A lab window. Formerly a portal to the outside world.
lab_window.summary: The is a Building which can only be found inside the Somnium Synthesizer story trait point of interest, which cannot be built. It is a type of Background Building, similar to Drywall.

pixel_pack.name: Pixel Pack
pixel_pack.description: Pixels can be designated a color when it receives a Green Signal and a different color when it deceives a Red Signal.Input from an Automation Wire controls the whole strip. Input from an Automation Ribbon can control individual pixels on the strip.
pixel_pack.summary: The Pixel Pack is a decorative item which has four one-tile-sized squares which can be toggled between two different colors using Automation. Each pixel can be controlled individually using an Automation Ribbon or an Automation Wire: If a Ribbon is used, each bit of the Ribbon's signal controls one corresponding square in the Pixel Pack, whereas if a Wire is used, all squares will be controlled using the Wire's signal.
pixel_pack.flavorText: Four pixels which can be individually designated different colors.
pixel_pack.autoInGreen: Display the configured Green Signal pixels
pixel_pack.autoInRed: Display the configured Red Signal pixels

tempshift_plate.name: Tempshift Plate
tempshift_plate.description: Accelerates or buffers Heat dispersal based on the construction material used.Has a small area of effect.
tempshift_plate.summary: The Tempshift Plate is a Background Building with the sole purpose of enhancing the exchange of heat. It has a size of 1x1 but exchanges heat within a space of 3x3 space around itself.
tempshift_plate.flavorText: The thermal properties of construction materials determine their heat retention.

wall.name: Wall
wall.description: The wall of a once-great scientific facility.
wall.summary: The is a Building which can only be found inside the Somnium Synthesizer story trait point of interest, which cannot be built. It is a type of Background Building, similar to Drywall.

window.name: Window
window.summary: Window is decorative objects found in the Ruins. They cannot be built but can be deconstructed. They are made of 25 kg Glass. They serve no purpose. They exist only in the Gravitas Lobby.

airflow_tile.name: Airflow Tile
airflow_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Blocks Liquid flow without obstructing Gas.
airflow_tile.summary: Airflow Tiles are a type of Tile and can be used to enclose rooms and support buildings. Unlike normal Tiles, gases can still pass through Airflow Tiles, at the cost of a small decor penalty in an immediate vicinity.
airflow_tile.flavorText: Building with airflow tile promotes better gas circulation within a colony.

automatic_dispenser.name: Automatic Dispenser
automatic_dispenser.description: Stores any Solid Materials delivered to it by Duplicants.Dumps stored materials back into the world when it receives a Green Signal.
automatic_dispenser.summary: The Automatic Dispenser is a variant of the Storage Bin that spits its contents on the ground to the side of it when given a Green automation signal. However, it has to be powered to hold items, and has a power draw of 60W and a heat output, unlike a basic storage container.
automatic_dispenser.flavorText: Automatic dispensers will store and drop resources in small quantities.

bunker_door.name: Bunker Door
bunker_door.description: Blocks Liquid and Gas flow, maintaining pressure between areas.Can withstand extremely high pressures and impacts.
bunker_door.summary: Bunker Door is a slow, double-sized airlock, that can be controlled with Automation signal, but cannot be used by dupes manually. Like Bunker Tiles, it does not take any damage from meteors, so it can be used to protect the surface against Meteor Showers using a Meteor Scanner. The door takes 44 seconds to open and 39 seconds to close when powered. It will still open and close without power, but it will take approximately 400 seconds. Thus they require at least 2 Scanners to close in time when powered and cannot close in time when unpowered. Using unpowered doors might be enough when rockets a
bunker_door.flavorText: A massive, slow-moving door which is nearly indestructible.

bunker_tile.name: Bunker Tile
bunker_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Can withstand extreme pressures and impacts.
bunker_tile.summary: Bunker Tiles are a Tile that doesn't take any damage from meteors, just like Bunker Doors, so it can be used to protect the surface against Meteor Showers.
bunker_tile.flavorText: Bunker tile can build strong shelters in otherwise dangerous environments.

carpeted_tile.name: Carpeted Tile
carpeted_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
carpeted_tile.summary: Carpeted Tiles provide large decor bonus and stress reduction at the cost of reducing Duplicant run speed by -25%.
carpeted_tile.flavorText: Carpeted tile remains decorative even when other tile is stacked atop it.

fire_pole.name: Fire Pole
fire_pole.description: Allows rapid Duplicant descent. Significantly slows upward climbing.
fire_pole.summary: Fire Pole functions similarly to ladders, the only difference being that downward movement is 400% as fast (for a duplicant with 0 Athletics) while upward movement is 75% slower. If a ladder and fire pole are both available to reach an area, a duplicant will prioritize using the pole to descend and prioritize the ladder to ascend.
fire_pole.flavorText: Fire poles provide quick downward travel, while ladders provide better upward movement.

gas_reservoir.name: Gas Reservoir
gas_reservoir.description: Stores any Gas resources piped into it.
gas_reservoir.summary: Gas Reservoir is a building that allows for 1000 kilograms of Gas storage.
gas_reservoir.flavorText: Reservoirs cannot receive manually delivered resources.

glass_backwall.name: Glass Backwall
glass_backwall.description: Prevents gas and liquid loss in space. Builds a transparent backwall behind buildings. Provides higher Decor than standard drywall
glass_backwall.flavorText: Glass Backwall adds a touch of elegance while sealing rooms from the vacuum of space.

insulated_door.name: Insulated Door
insulated_door.description: Significantly reduces Temperature exchange between climate-controlled rooms.
insulated_door.summary: Insulated Doors create an airtight seal that maintains temperature, atmosphere and pressure between areas while closed. They cost and 2 Reed Fiber or Feather Fiber to build. Insulated doors take 4 seconds to open, and another 4 to close. They cannot be powered or sped up.

insulated_tile.name: Insulated Tile
insulated_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms. Reduces heat transfer between walls, retaining ambient heat in an area.
insulated_tile.summary: Insulated Tiles (not to be confused with the resource ) are used as wall and floor tile to build rooms and manage heat at the cost of -5 Decor and double the materials compared to normal Tiles. Specifically, it reduces the Thermal Conductivity of the material used in its construction to \left(\frac{2}{255}\right)^2 (e.g. Igneous Rock has thermal conductivity of 2, but 0.000123 when built as an Insulated Tile), however for some reason the game displays the thermal conductivity as being reduced to 1/100th.
insulated_tile.flavorText: The low thermal conductivity of insulated tiles slows any heat passing through them.

ladder.name: Ladder
ladder.summary: Ladders are used to grant access to the different floors of the colony, as well as functioning as scaffolding structures for various projects throughout the base.

liquid_reservoir.name: Liquid Reservoir
liquid_reservoir.description: Stores any Liquid resources piped into it.
liquid_reservoir.summary: Liquid Reservoir is a building that allows for 5 ton of Liquid storage.
liquid_reservoir.flavorText: Reservoirs cannot receive manually delivered resources.

manual_airlock.name: Manual Airlock
manual_airlock.description: Blocks Liquid and Gas flow, maintaining pressure between areas.Sets Duplicant Access Permissions for area restriction.
manual_airlock.summary: The Manual Airlock creates an airtight seal that maintains atmosphere and pressure between areas.
manual_airlock.flavorText: Wild Critters cannot pass through doors.Airlocks can quarter off dangerous areas and prevent gases from seeping into the colony.

mechanized_airlock.name: Mechanized Airlock
mechanized_airlock.description: Blocks Liquid and Gas flow, maintaining pressure between areas.Sets Duplicant Access Permissions for area restriction.
mechanized_airlock.summary: The Mechanized Airlock creates an airtight seal that maintains atmosphere and pressure between areas when closed. It can also control Duplicant movement by acting as a one-way door, or by blocking specific dupes from passing.
mechanized_airlock.flavorText: Functions as a Manual Airlock when no Power is available. Mechanized Airlocks open and close more quickly than other types of door.

mesh_tile.name: Mesh Tile
mesh_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Does not obstruct Liquid or Gas flow.
mesh_tile.summary: Mesh Tiles are a type of Tile and can be used to enclose rooms. Mesh Tiles do not obstruct the flow of liquids or gasses, but still can support buildings, at the cost of a small decor penalty in an immediate vicinity.
mesh_tile.flavorText: Mesh tile can be used to make Duplicant pathways in areas where liquid flows.

metal_tile.name: Metal Tile
metal_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Significantly increases Duplicant runspeed.
metal_tile.summary: Metal Tiles are a type of tile that is constructed from Refined Metal. Metal tiles provide a +50% runspeed bonus to Duplicants moving across them.
metal_tile.flavorText: Heat travels much more quickly through metal tiles than other types of flooring.

mini_pod.name: Mini-Pod
mini_pod.description: A portable Bioprinter that produces new Duplicants or Care Packages containing resources.Only one Printing Pod or Mini-Pod is permitted per Planetoid.
mini_pod.summary: The Mini-Pod follows the same rules as the Printing Pod. All the Mini-Pods share one cooldown timer with the Printing Pod. This means printing a Duplicant or a Care Package will reset the cooldown timer on the main Printing Pod and other Mini-Pods.
mini_pod.flavorText: A quick and easy substitute, though it'll never live up to the original.

plastic_ladder.name: Plastic Ladder
plastic_ladder.summary: Plastic Ladders boost duplicant climbing speed compared to Ladders. They are used to grant access to the different floors of the colony, as well as functioning as scaffolding structures for various projects throughout the base.
plastic_ladder.flavorText: Plastic ladders are mildly antiseptic and can help limit the spread of germs in a colony.

plastic_tile.name: Plastic Tile
plastic_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.
plastic_tile.summary: Plastic Tiles are a type of tile that is made of Plastics, and the most commonly, the basic Plastic.
plastic_tile.flavorText: Significantly increases Duplicant runspeed.Plastic tile is mildly antiseptic and can help limit the spread of germs in a colony.

pneumatic_door.name: Pneumatic Door
pneumatic_door.description: Encloses areas without blocking Liquid or Gas flow.Sets Duplicant Access Permissions for area restriction.
pneumatic_door.summary: The Pneumatic Door encloses rooms without blocking the flow of Gases or Liquids. They open faster than unpowered airlocks and are useful to limit the movement of Duplicants and Critters, so they stay in their preferred Biome or Stable, and to designate boundaries of morale-boosting rooms that dupes can cross.
pneumatic_door.flavorText: Wild Critters cannot pass through doors.Door controls can be used to prevent Duplicants from entering restricted areas.

rubber_tile.name: Rubber Tile
rubber_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Provides an anti-slip work surface and effective Insulation.
rubber_tile.flavorText: Rubber tiles are designed to resist damage from liquid pressure.

smart_storage_bin.name: Smart Storage Bin
smart_storage_bin.description: Stores the resources of your choosing.Sends a Green Signal when bin is full.
smart_storage_bin.summary: Smart Storage Bin is a building that can store 20 tons (20,000 kilograms) of material. Unlike regular Storage Bins, they can output Automation signals when powered.
smart_storage_bin.flavorText: Smart storage bins allow for the automation of resource organization based on type and mass.

snow_tile.name: Snow Tile
snow_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.
snow_tile.summary: Snow Tiles are used as basic wall and floor tiles in sub-zero climates. They are ideal for use in situations where Raw Minerals are scarce or unavailable, and where Snow is plentiful.
snow_tile.flavorText: Insulates rooms to reduce Heat loss in cold climates.Snow tiles have low thermal conductivity, but will melt if temperatures get too high.

storage_bin.name: Storage Bin
storage_bin.description: Stores the Solid resources of your choosing.
storage_bin.summary: The Storage Bin stores the Solid resources of your choosing.
storage_bin.flavorText: Resources left on the floor become "debris" and lower decor when not put away.

storage_tile.name: Storage Tile
storage_tile.description: Used to built the wall and floors of rooms.Provides built-in storage for small spaces.
storage_tile.summary: Storage Tiles store solid resources of your choosing.
storage_tile.flavorText: Storage tiles keep selected non-edible solids out of the way.

tile.name: Tile
tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.
tile.summary: Tiles are used as wall and floor segments to build rooms. Duplicants get a 25% runspeed bonus on Tile.
tile.flavorText: Increases Duplicant runspeed. Tile can be used to bridge gaps and get to unreachable areas.

transit_tube.name: Transit Tube
transit_tube.description: Quickly transports Duplicants from a Transit Tube Access to the tube's end.Only transports Duplicants.
transit_tube.summary: Transit Tube is the primary components of a Tube System to rapidly transport Duplicants from place to place within your colony. Tubes can only be entered with a Transit Tube Access and completely shield a Duplicant from whatever hazards the tube is run through whilst the Duplicant is inside. Like Heavi-Watt Wires, Transit Tubes cannot cross tiles, but a Transit Tube Crossing may be used in place of a tile.
transit_tube.flavorText: Duplicants will only exit a transit tube when a safe landing area is available beneath it.

transit_tube_access.name: Transit Tube Access
transit_tube_access.description: Allows Duplicants to enter the connected Transit Tube system.Stops drawing Power once fully charged.
transit_tube_access.summary: Transit Tube Access building functions as the primary way for Duplicants to enter Transit Tubes. Duplicants will stand at the Access Point for a brief period before they are sent up into the tubes; additional Duplicants will queue up behind one-another and wait their turn.
transit_tube_access.flavorText: Duplicants require access points to enter tubes, but not to exit them.

transit_tube_crossing.name: Transit Tube Crossing
transit_tube_crossing.description: Allows Transit Tubes to be run through walls and floor tile.Functions as regular tile.
transit_tube_crossing.summary: Transit Tube Crossing allows a Tube System to go through walls. The Transit Tube Crossing takes the place of a wall tile and then connects to Transit Tubes on both of its sides.
transit_tube_crossing.flavorText: Tube crossings can run transit tubes through walls without leaking gas or liquid.

wicker_door.name: Wicker Door
wicker_door.description: Encloses areas without blocking Liquid or Gas flow.Wild Critters cannot pass through doors.
wicker_door.summary: The Wicker Door is a door that allows Liquid and Gas flow, similar to the Pneumatic Door. It may be preferable over its counterpart when Wood is more plentiful than Metal Ore. Unlike the Pneumatic Door, it does not support automation.
wicker_door.flavorText: A breezy wooden door combines style and function.

window_tile.name: Window Tile
window_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Allows Light and Decor values to pass through.
window_tile.summary: Window Tile is made from Transparent materials. Unlike normal tiles, they allow Light and Decor to pass through.
window_tile.flavorText: Window tiles provide a barrier against liquid and gas and are completely transparent.

wood_tile.name: Wood Tile
wood_tile.description: Used to build the walls and floors of rooms.Provides good insulation and boosts Decor, contributing to Morale.
wood_tile.summary: Wood Tiles are tiles built out of Wood. They can be used as walls and floors.
wood_tile.flavorText: Rooms built with wood tile are cozy and pleasant.

deep_fryer.name: Deep Fryer
deep_fryer.description: Uses Tallow to cook a wide variety of improved Foods.Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
deep_fryer.summary: The Deep Fryer allows Duplicants to cook high-quality food using Tallow. Duplicants will not fabricate unless recipes are queued. It requires Grilling skill and to be built inside a Kitchen.
deep_fryer.flavorText: Everything tastes better when it's deep-fried.

ice_liquefier.name: Ice Liquefier
ice_liquefier.description: Consumes Wood to melt Ice into Water, which can be bottled for transport.
ice_liquefier.summary: The Ice Liquefier turns Ice into Water, in batches of 100 kg every 5 seconds, at the cost of Wood. It is mostly useful for Advanced Research and Wash Basins in Frosty Planet starts where Wood and Ice are abundant but liquid water is hard to come by.
ice_liquefier.flavorText: The water never gets hot enough to burn the tongue.
ice_liquefier.effects: Melting Rate: 20 kg/sStorage Capacity: 500 kg water, 1000 kg ice, 1000 kg wood

mercury_ceiling_light.name: Mercury Ceiling Light
mercury_ceiling_light.description: The Mercury Ceiling Lights convert Mercury and small amounts of Power into very bright, concentrated rays of Light.

oxylite_sconce.name: Oxylite Sconce
oxylite_sconce.description: Stores a small chunk of Oxylite which gradually releases Oxygen into the environment.
oxylite_sconce.summary: The Oxylite Sconce is used to store Oxylite in order to let it off-gas Oxygen.
oxylite_sconce.flavorText: Sconces prevent diffused oxygen from being wasted inside storage bins.
oxylite_sconce.effects: Storage Capacity: Oxygen: (average from the Oxylite)

wood_block.name: Wood Block
wood_block.description: Moderately increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
wood_block.summary: The Wood Block is a decorative building that does not need a floor, ceiling or wall as its base, and only occupies 1x1 of space. Each artistic quality provides an additional decor bonus: +4 for Crude, +8 for Quaint, +12 for Masterpiece.
wood_block.flavorText: A great fit for smaller spaces.

wood_heater.name: Wood Heater
wood_heater.description: Consumes Wood in order to Heat chilly surroundings.
wood_heater.summary: The Wood Heater is a Utility Building that consumes Wood to heat up its environment and to grant Duplicants temporary immunity against some cold-related debuffs.
wood_heater.flavorText: Wood heaters dry out soggy feet and help Duplicants forget how cold they are.
wood_heater.effects: Frost ResistanceCarbon Dioxide: +4000 mg/s @ + (Input-dependent) Light range: 6 tilesBrightness: 450 Lux

dev_generator.name: Dev Generator
dev_generator.description: Generating testing power for late nights.
dev_generator.summary: Dev Generator is a Power Generator in testing version which gives free and nearly infinite energy.

dev_life_support.name: Dev Life Support
dev_life_support.summary: Dev Life Support is a Medicine building which gives free oxygen and gets rid of carbon dioxide produced from dupes. Oxygen produced has a constant temperature of on a square of 15x15 around it.

food_building.name: Food (Building)
food_building.description: Food buildings focus on food production and storage. Food is one of the most important resources in Oxygen Not Included.

airborne_critter_condo.name: Airborne Critter Condo
airborne_critter_condo.description: A hanging respite area for adult Pufts, Gassy Moos and Shine Bugs.
airborne_critter_condo.summary: The gives flying critters a way to relax to improve their happiness.
airborne_critter_condo.flavorText: Triggers natural nesting instincts and improves critter's moods.

airborne_critter_trap.name: Airborne Critter Trap
airborne_critter_trap.description: Attracts and captures airborne Critters.Only Duplicants with the Critter Ranching I skill can arm this trap. It's reusable!
airborne_critter_trap.summary: Airborne Critter Trap is a building that will catch flying critters that fly into the trap. Duplicants can then take the bagged creature to a nearby Critter Drop-Off.
airborne_critter_trap.flavorText: It needs to be armed prior to use.

aquatic_critter_condo.name: Aquatic Critter Condo
aquatic_critter_condo.description: A fancy respite area for adult Pokeshells and Pacu.
aquatic_critter_condo.summary: Aquatic Critter Condo gives aquatic critters critters a way to relax to improve their happiness.
aquatic_critter_condo.flavorText: Even wild critters are happier after they've had a little R&R.

aquatic_critter_fountain.name: Aquatic Critter Fountain
aquatic_critter_fountain.description: Dispenses Brackene or Squid Ink to aquatic Critters. Accessing the fountain significantly improves Critters' moods.
aquatic_critter_fountain.flavorText: A soothing sip of brackene helps critters put up with crowded quarters

critter_condo.name: Critter Condo
critter_condo.description: Provides a comfortable lounge area that boosts Critter happiness.
critter_condo.summary: The Critter Condo gives land-based critters a way to relax to improve their happiness.
critter_condo.flavorText: It's nice to have nice things.

critter_drop_off.name: Critter Drop-Off
critter_drop_off.description: Releases trapped Critters back into the world.Can be used multiple times.
critter_drop_off.summary: The Critter Drop-Off is a building that allows Duplicants to move a captured Critter to a different location. Creatures can be captured using a Critter Trap or by being wrangled directly by a Duplicant with the Ranching skill.
critter_drop_off.flavorText: Duplicants automatically bring captured critters to these relocation points for release.

critter_feeder.name: Critter Feeder
critter_feeder.description: Automatically dispenses food for hungry Critters.
critter_feeder.summary: The Critter Feeder is a building that automatically dispenses food for hungry Critters. Critters tend to stay close to their food source and will wander less when given a Feeder. It can store up to 2000 kg of critter food. Critters will prioritize eating from a Critter Feeder over debris.
critter_feeder.flavorText: Critters tend to stay close to their food source and will wander less when given a Feeder.

critter_fountain.name: Critter Fountain
critter_fountain.description: Dispenses Brackene or Squid Ink to a wide variety of Critters.
critter_fountain.summary: Accessing the fountain significantly improves Critters' moods.
critter_fountain.flavorText: Providing beverages to critters makes them feel taken care of.

critter_lure.name: Critter Lure
critter_lure.description: Attracts one type of air born critter.
critter_lure.summary: Critter Lures can attract flying critters.
critter_lure.flavorText: Lures can relocate Pufts or Shinebugs to specific locations in your base.

critter_pick_up.name: Critter Pick-Up
critter_pick_up.description: Ensures the prompt relocation of Critters that exceed the maximum amout set.Monitoring and pick-up are limited to the specified species.
critter_pick_up.summary: Critter Pick-Up allows the automation of critter wrangling by duplicants.

critter_trap.name: Critter Trap
critter_trap.description: Captures a living Critter for transport.Only Duplicants with the Critter Ranching I skill can arm this trap. It's reusable!
critter_trap.summary: Not to be confused with the Saturn Critter Trap.
critter_trap.flavorText: It's designed for land critters, but flopping fish sometimes find their way in too.

dehydrator.name: Dehydrator
dehydrator.description: Uses low, even heat to eliminate moisture from eligible Foods and render them shelf-stable.Dehydrated meals must be processed at the Rehydrator before they can be eaten.
dehydrator.summary: The Dehydrator is a building the allows the creation of dehydrated food packs.
dehydrator.flavorText: Some of the eliminated liquid inevitably ends up on the floor.
dehydrator.requires: Natural Gas: -20g/sDuplicant operation (for loading and unloading)

egg_cracker.name: Egg Cracker
egg_cracker.description: Converts viable Critter eggs into cooking ingredients.Cracked Eggs cannot hatch. Duplicants will not crack eggs unless tasks are queued.
egg_cracker.summary: Egg Cracker cracks open a critter egg for use as a cooking ingredient, converting it into Raw Egg and Egg Shell. Different types of critter eggs produce differing amounts of these items. Shine Bug eggs and its variations also drop some Resin when cracked.
egg_cracker.flavorText: Raw eggs are an ingredient in certain high quality food recipes.
egg_cracker.effects: Varied amounts of Raw Egg, Egg Shell & Resin

electric_grill.name: Electric Grill
electric_grill.description: Cooks a wide variety of improved Foods.Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
electric_grill.summary: The Electric Grill allows Duplicants to create some advanced and intermediary food options for the Colony. Duplicants will not fabricate unless recipes are queued. It requires the Grilling Skill and takes 50 seconds.
electric_grill.flavorText: Proper cooking eliminates foodborne disease and produces tasty, stress-relieving meals.

farm_station.name: Farm Station
farm_station.description: Produces Micronutrient Fertilizer to increase Plant growth rates.Assigned Duplicants must possess the Crop Tending trait.
farm_station.summary: Farm Station produces Micronutrient Fertilizer to give Farmer's Touch modifier to one plant, which doubles the plant growth speed for 1 cycle + 0.1 cycles for every level of the dupe's Agriculture skill (e.g. a Farmer with +15 Agriculture will provide a 2.5 cycle Farmer's Touch modifier).
farm_station.flavorText: This building is a necessary component of the Greenhouse room.This station only has an effect on crops grown within the same room.
farm_station.requires: Fertilizer: -5 kg per useDuplicant operationCrop Tending skill

farm_tile.name: Farm Tile
farm_tile.description: Grows one Plant from a Seed.Can be used as floor tile and rotated before construction.
farm_tile.summary: Farm Tiles are a type of Planter that grows a single Plant when sown with a seed. They can be used as floor tile and rotated before construction.
farm_tile.flavorText: Duplicants can deliver fertilizer and liquids to farm tiles, accelerating plant growth.

fish_feeder.name: Fish Feeder
fish_feeder.description: Automatically dispenses stored Critter food into the area below.Dispenses continuously as food is consumed.
fish_feeder.summary: is used to feed many aquatic critters: Pacus, Jawbos, Blowters, Beakons, Seaquines or Orehulls. Fish that eat from the will receive the "Ate From Feeder" effect after each use, which grants -10% Wildness/cycle for 2 cycles. Note: wild critters always have +5% Wildness/cycle, so the effective Wildness/cycle is -5% for the purposes of taming.
fish_feeder.flavorText: Build this feeder above a body of water to feed the fish within.

fish_pick_up.name: Fish Pick-Up
fish_pick_up.description: Ensures the prompt capture of aquatic Critters that exceed the maximum amount set. Monitoring and pick-up are limited to the specified species. Must be built above a Liquid pool.
fish_pick_up.flavorText: Duplicants will automatically wrangle excess fish from the pool below.

fish_release.name: Fish Release
fish_release.description: Releases trapped Pacu back into the world.Can be used multiple times.
fish_release.summary: Fish Release is used to release fish (i.e. Pacus) that were trapped with a Fish Trap.
fish_release.flavorText: A fish release must be built above liquid to prevent released fish from suffocating.

fish_trap.name: Fish Trap
fish_trap.description: Attracts and traps swimming Pacu.Only Duplicants with the Critter Ranching I skill can arm this trap. It's reusable!
fish_trap.summary: Fish Trap is a trap used to capture fish (i.e. Pacus and their fry). Once captured, a Duplicant will bring it to a Fish Release.
fish_trap.flavorText: Trapped fish will automatically be bagged for transport.

gas_range.name: Gas Range
gas_range.description: Cooks a wide variety of quality Foods.Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
gas_range.summary: Gas Range allows Duplicants to create the best food options for the Colony. Duplicants will not fabricate unless recipes are queued. It requires Grilling skill and takes 50s.
gas_range.flavorText: Luxury meals increase Duplicants morale and prevents them from becoming stressed.

hydroponic_farm.name: Hydroponic Farm
hydroponic_farm.description: Grows one Plant from a Seed.Can be used as floor tile and rotated before construction.
hydroponic_farm.summary: Hydroponic Farms are a type of Planter that grows a single Plant when sown with a seed. It can be used as floor tile and rotated (upside-down) before construction.
hydroponic_farm.flavorText: Must be irrigated through Liquid Piping.Hydroponic farms reduce Duplicant traffic by automating irrigating crops.

incubator.name: Incubator
incubator.description: Incubates Critter eggs until ready to hatch.Assigned Duplicants must possess the Critter Wrangling trait.
incubator.summary: The Incubator is a building that incubates the eggs of Critters until they are ready to hatch. Eggs inside an Incubator can be lullabied by a Duplicant rancher, giving the egg a buff for one cycle that increases the incubation speed by up to 400% (5 times faster). For example, Pacu eggs naturally take 5 cycles to hatch at 20% incubation per cycle, and the "lullabied" status adds 80% to this rate, guaranteeing the Pacu egg to hatch in 1 cycle.
incubator.flavorText: Incubators can maintain the ideal internal conditions for several species of critter egg.
incubator.requires: Critter EggsDuplicant operationCritter Ranching I skill

microbe_musher.name: Microbe Musher
microbe_musher.description: Produces low quality Food using common ingredients.Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
microbe_musher.summary: The Microbe Musher is a building used to produce some starting and intermediary Food. Recipes must be assigned in order for Duplicants to work.
microbe_musher.flavorText: Musher recipes will keep Duplicants fed, but may impact health and morale over time.

mini_fridge.name: Mini Fridge
mini_fridge.description: Stores Food at an ideal Temperature to prevent spoilage.

planter_box.name: Planter Box
planter_box.description: Grows one Plant from a Seed.
planter_box.summary: Planter Boxes are used to grow Plants from seeds that can be found in your world either by digging up or harvesting pre-existing plants, or by digging up a Buried Object. Using a Planter Box decreases growth time compared to natural growth, but requires additional care.
planter_box.flavorText: Domestically grown seeds mature more quickly than wild plants.

ration_box.name: Ration Box
ration_box.description: Stores a small amount of Food.Food must be delivered to boxes by Duplicants.
ration_box.summary: The Ration Box is a storage unit used to store and maintain your Food. One is always generated to the left of the Printing Pod at the start of every game with 16,000 kcal of Nutrient Bars inside, and its filter set to Nutrient Bars only. It can store 150 kg of food.
ration_box.flavorText: Ration boxes keep food safe from hungry critters, but don't slow food spoilage.

refrigerator.name: Refrigerator
refrigerator.description: Stores Food at an ideal Temperature to prevent spoilage.
refrigerator.summary: The Refrigerator is a structure that drastically increases the shelf-life of food by cooling its contents to , at the cost of electricity and heating the nearby environment. Refrigerators only draw a full 120W if actively cooling food down to the target temp. If all food inside is already at , then it enters a "Power Saving" mode that only draws 20W. Even if the contents have not yet reached , food inside a powered Refrigerator is always considered to be refrigerated.
refrigerator.flavorText: Food spoilage can be slowed by ambient conditions as well as refrigerators.

rehydrator.name: Rehydrator
rehydrator.description: Restores moisture to convert shelf-stable packaged meals into edible food.
rehydrator.summary: The Rehydrator is a building the allows to convert dehydrated food packs into edible food.
rehydrator.flavorText: Rehydrated food is nutritous and only slightly less delicious.

smoker.name: Smoker
smoker.description: Cooks improved food over low, slow heat. Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
smoker.summary: Smoker is a building that accepts a delivery and begins working by itself, like a Kiln. After the recipe is complete, a duplicant with the Grilling Skill needs to empty it before a new recipe can be processed.
smoker.flavorText: With a little patience, even tough meat can become deliciously edible.

spice_grinder.name: Spice Grinder
spice_grinder.description: Produces ingredients that add benefits to foods prepared at skilled cooking stations.
spice_grinder.summary: Spice Grinder allows Duplicants to spice up food to provide bonus for food. Duplicants will spice food as an errand. This building is required to be built in the Kitchen room to be functional.
spice_grinder.flavorText: Crushed seeds and other edibles make excellent meal-enhancing additives.
spice_grinder.requires: Duplicant operationGrilling II skillInside a Kitchen

sushi_bar.name: Sushi Bar
sushi_bar.description: Prepares a variety of Uncooked Seafood. Duplicants will not fabricate items unless recipes are queued.
sushi_bar.flavorText: Expert chefs let the ingredients speak for themselves.

wall_planter.name: Wall Planter
wall_planter.description: Grows one extra-large aquatic Plant from a Seed. It also doubles as a wall tile.
wall_planter.flavorText: Vertical farming makes oversized organisms more accessible in limited space.

wide_hydroponic_farm.name: Wide Hydroponic Farm
wide_hydroponic_farm.description: Grows one extra-large aquatic Plant from a Seed. Can be used as floor tile. Must be irrigated through Liquid Piping.
wide_hydroponic_farm.flavorText: Enables Duplicants to accelerate growth rates for oversized plants too.

aero_pot.name: Aero Pot
aero_pot.description: Houses a single Plant when sown with a Seed.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
aero_pot.summary: The Aero Pot can host a decorative plant which will increase Decor in the surrounding area. However, if the plant's needs are not met (and the plant becomes Stifled), it will lower decor instead.
aero_pot.flavorText: Must be hung from a ceiling.Aero pots can be hung from the ceiling and have extremely high decor values.

arcade_cabinet.name: Arcade Cabinet
arcade_cabinet.description: Allows Duplicants to play video games on their breaks.Increases Duplicant Morale.
arcade_cabinet.summary: The Arcade Cabinet is a recreational building under the Furniture tab. Arcade Cabinets are used during Downtime. Up to 2 Duplicants can use it at the same time.
arcade_cabinet.flavorText: Komet Kablam-O! For up to two players.

beach_chair.name: Beach Chair
beach_chair.description: Duplicants can relax by lounging in Sunlight.Increases Duplicants Morale.
beach_chair.summary: The Beach Chair is used during downtime to improve a duplicant's morale. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a recreational room.
beach_chair.flavorText: Soak up some relaxing sun rays.

blank_canvas.name: Blank Canvas
blank_canvas.description: Increases Decor, contributing to Morale. Must be painted by a Duplicant.
blank_canvas.summary: The Blank Canvas is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be painted on to provide a further bonus. The quality of the painting influences this bonus with higher quality paintings from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.
blank_canvas.flavorText: Once built, a Duplicant can paint a blank canvas to produce a decorative painting.

ceiling_light.name: Ceiling Light
ceiling_light.description: Provides Light when Powered.Duplicants can operate buldings more quickly when the building is lit.
ceiling_light.summary: The Ceiling Light improves Decor Opinion and reduces Stress by providing Light.
ceiling_light.flavorText: Light reduces Duplicant stress and is required to grow certain plants.

ceiling_trim.name: Ceiling Trim
ceiling_trim.description: Used to decorate the ceilings of rooms.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
ceiling_trim.summary: Ceiling Trims are a decorative building for ceilings. It provides a small decor value of +5. Building it with Sandstone provides a +10% decor bonus for 5.5 decor, and building it with Granite or Ceramic provides a +20% decor bonus for 6 decor.
ceiling_trim.flavorText: Ceiling trim is a purely decorative addition to one's overhead area.

comfy_bed.name: Comfy Bed
comfy_bed.description: Provides a sleeping area for one Duplicant and restores additional Stamina.Duplicants will automatically sleep in their assigned beds at night.
comfy_bed.summary: The Comfy Bed provides a sleeping area for one Duplicant and restores additional stamina, providing more stamina regeneration than a Cot. It can be used to create a Luxury Barracks or a Private Bedroom which improve morale.
comfy_bed.flavorText: Duplicants prefer comfy beds to cots and gain more stamina from sleeping in them.

communal_table.name: Communal Table
communal_table.description: Gives three Duplicants a place to eat.Sharing a meal with one or more companions provides a Morale boost.
communal_table.summary: The Communal Table is a furniture item to which Duplicants can use to consume their food. Communal Tables are the main component for the Banquet Hall Room. They can also satisfy the Dining Table room requirement for both a Mess Hall and a Great Hall. It can store up to Table Salt and both consumes Table Salt and grants the duplicant +1 morale. Eating food alongside one or more duplicants grants an additional +1 morale bonus.
communal_table.flavorText: Given the option, Duplicants prefer to dine with friends.

corner_trim.name: Corner Trim
corner_trim.description: Used to decorate the ceiling corners of rooms.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
corner_trim.summary: Corner Trims are a decorative building for ceiling corners. It provides a small decor value of +5. Building it with Sandstone or Granite can provide a +10% bonus for 5.5 decor or a +20% bonus for 6 decor accordingly.
corner_trim.flavorText: Corner trim is a purely decorative addition for ceiling corners.

cot.name: Cot
cot.description: Gives one Duplicant a place to sleep.Duplicants will automatically return to their cots to sleep at night.
cot.summary: The Cot reduces stress accumulation and increases productivity by giving Duplicants a place to rest.
cot.flavorText: Duplicants without a bed will develop sore backs from sleeping on the floor.

display_shelf.name: Display Shelf
display_shelf.description: Displays cherished items and increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be installed on a back wall.
display_shelf.summary: The Display Shelf is a decorative Building that can be configured to display up to 1 kg or 1 unit of various resources. A Display Shelf with nothing on it will grant +5 Decor with a radius of 1 tile. Display Shelves with items on it gain additional Decor depending on the item being displayed:
display_shelf.flavorText: It looks great even when it's empty.

espresso_machine.name: Espresso Machine
espresso_machine.description: Provides refreshment for Duplicants on their breaks.Increases Duplicant Morale.
espresso_machine.summary: The Espresso Machine is a recreational building. A single Duplicant can use the Espresso Machine during downtime. It consumes 1 kg Pincha Peppernut and 1 kg Water per use, and provides a buff which raises Morale by 4 and Athletics by 1. The Water is supplied via a Liquid Pipe.
espresso_machine.flavorText: A shot of espresso helps Duplicants relax after a long day.
espresso_machine.requires: Pincha Peppernut: 1 kg per useWater: 1 kg per use

flower_pot.name: Flower Pot
flower_pot.description: Houses a single Plant when sown with a Seed. Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
flower_pot.summary: The Flower Pot, previously known as the Flower Vase, increases Decor opinion and reduces Duplicant's Stress. They can host a decorative Plant which will increase Decor in the surrounding area. However, if the plant's needs aren't met (and the plant becomes Stifled), it will lower decor instead.
flower_pot.flavorText: Flower pots allow decorative plants to be moved to new locations.

fossil_block.name: Fossil Block
fossil_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
fossil_block.summary: The Fossil Block is a large decorative building. Each artistic quality provides an additional decor bonus: +5 for Crude, +10 for Quaint, +15 for Masterpiece.
fossil_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative sculptures.

glass_jelly_ceiling_light.name: Glass Jelly Ceiling Light
glass_jelly_ceiling_light.description: Provides Light when Powered. Increases Duplicant workspeed withing light radius.
glass_jelly_ceiling_light.flavorText: It's an attractive way to add overhead lightiing in aquatic spaces

hanging_fossil_block.name: Hanging Fossil Block
hanging_fossil_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
hanging_fossil_block.summary: The Hanging Fossil Block is a large decorative building. Each artistic quality provides an additional decor bonus: +5 for Crude, +10 for Quaint, +15 for Masterpiece.
hanging_fossil_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative ceiling sculptures.

hanging_pot.name: Hanging Pot
hanging_pot.description: Houses a single Plant when sown with a Seed.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
hanging_pot.summary: The Hanging Pot can host a decorative plant which will increase Decor in the surrounding area. However, if the plant's needs are not met (and the plant becomes Stifled), it will lower decor instead.
hanging_pot.flavorText: Must be hung from a ceiling.Hanging pots can add some decor to a room, without blocking buildings on the floor.

hot_tub.name: Hot Tub
hot_tub.description: Requires Pipes to and from tub and Power to run the jets.Water must be a comfortable temperature and will cool rapidly.Increases Duplicant Morale.
hot_tub.summary: The Hot Tub is used during downtime to improve a Duplicant's morale. A Duplicant will spend 90 seconds (~4 schedule blocks) in the hot tub. Up to 4 Duplicants are able to use the Hot Tub at the same time. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a recreational room.
hot_tub.flavorText: Relaxes Duplicants with massaging jets of heated liquid.
hot_tub.requires: Preferred Room: Recreation RoomMinimum Water Temperature: Water: ? kg/sBleach Stone: 0.1167kg/s

ice_block.name: Ice Block
ice_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
ice_block.summary: The Ice Block is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be sculpted to provide a further bonus. The quality of the sculpture influences this bonus with higher quality sculptures from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.

juicer.name: Juicer
juicer.description: Provides refreshment for Duplicants on their breaks.Drinking juice increases Duplicant Morale.
juicer.summary: The Juicer is used during downtime to improve a Duplicant's morale by 4 and Strength by 1. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a Recreation Room.
juicer.flavorText: Fruity juice can really brighten a Duplicant's breaktime
juicer.requires: Preferred Room: Recreation RoomMushroom: 125 g per useBristle Berry: 375 g per useMeal Lice: 833 g per useWater: 1000 g per use
juicer.storage: Mushroom: 10 kgBristle Berry: 10 kgMeal Lice: 10 kgWater: 2000 g

jukebot.name: Jukebot
jukebot.description: Plays music for Duplicants to dance to on their breaks.Increases Duplicant Morale.
jukebot.summary: The Jukebot is a recreational building under the Furniture tab. Jukebots are used during Downtime. Up to 5 Duplicants can use it at the same time.
jukebot.flavorText: Dancing helps Duplicants get their innermost feelings out.

ladder_bed.name: Ladder Bed
ladder_bed.description: Provides a sleeping area for one Duplicant and also functions as a ladder.Duplicants will automatically sleep in their assigned beds at night.
ladder_bed.summary: The Ladder Bed is a combination of a Cot and a Ladder.
ladder_bed.flavorText: Duplicant's sleep will be interrupted if another Duplicant uses the ladder.

lamp.name: Lamp
lamp.description: Provides Light when Powered.Duplicants can operate buldings more quickly when the building is lit.
lamp.summary: The Lamp improves Decor and reduces Stress by providing Light. It emits Light from its upper tile.
lamp.flavorText: Any building's light emitting radius can be viewed in the light overlay.

landscape_canvas.name: Landscape Canvas
landscape_canvas.description: Moderately increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be painted by a Duplicant.
landscape_canvas.summary: The Landscape Canvas is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be painted on to provide extra.
landscape_canvas.flavorText: Once built, a Duplicant can paint a blank canvas to produce a decorative painting.

large_sculpting_block.name: Large Sculpting Block
large_sculpting_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
large_sculpting_block.summary: The Large Sculpting Block is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be sculpted to provide a further bonus. The quality of the sculpture influences this bonus with higher quality sculptures from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.
large_sculpting_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative sculptures.

marble_block.name: Marble Block
marble_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
marble_block.summary: The Marble Block is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be sculpted to provide a further bonus. The quality of the sculpture influences this bonus with higher quality sculptures from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.
marble_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative sculptures.

mechanical_surfboard.name: Mechanical Surfboard
mechanical_surfboard.description: Increases Duplicant Morale.Some Water gets splashed on the floor during use.
mechanical_surfboard.summary: The Mechanical Surfboard is used during downtime to improve a duplicants' morale and reduce stress. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a Recreation Room.
mechanical_surfboard.flavorText: Mechanical waves make for radical relaxation time.

mess_table.name: Mess Table
mess_table.description: Gives one Duplicant a place to eat.Duplicants will automatically eat at their assigned table when hungry.
mess_table.summary: The Mess Table is a furniture item where Duplicants consume their food. Mess Tables are the main component for the Mess Hall and Great Hall rooms. It can store up to 5 g Table Salt, and when a Duplicant sits down to consume their food, they will also consume 1 g Table Salt which grants the duplicant +1 morale per use.
mess_table.flavorText: Duplicants prefer to dine at a table, rather than eat off the floor.

metal_block.name: Metal Block
metal_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
metal_block.summary: The Metal Block is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be sculpted to provide a further bonus. The quality of the sculpture influences this bonus with higher quality sculptures from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.
metal_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative sculptures.

monument_base.name: Monument Base
monument_base.description: Builds the bottom section of a Great Monument.Can be customized.A Great Monument must be built to achieve the Colonize Imperative.
monument_base.summary: The Monument Base is a decorative building that comprises the Great Monument.
monument_base.flavorText: The base of a monument must be constructed first.

monument_midsection.name: Monument Midsection
monument_midsection.description: Builds the middle section of a Great Monument.Can be customized.A Great Monument must be built to achieve the Colonize Imperative.
monument_midsection.summary: The Monument Midsection is a decorative building that comprises the Great Monument. The building can only be constructed on top of the Monument Base.Must be built overlapping a MonumentMiddle
monument_midsection.flavorText: Customized sections of a Great Monument can be mixed and matched.

monument_top.name: Monument Top
monument_top.description: Builds the top section of a Great Monument.Can be customized.A Great Monument must be built to achieve the Colonize Imperative.
monument_top.summary: The Monument Top is a decorative building that comprises the Great Monument. The building can only be constructed on top of the Monument Midsection.Must be built overlapping a MonumentTop
monument_top.flavorText: Building a Great Monument will declare to the universe that this hunk of rock is your own.

park_sign.name: Park Sign
park_sign.description: Classifies an area as a Park or Nature Reserve.
park_sign.summary: The Park Sign is used to designate two morale-boosting rooms, the Park and the Nature Reserve.
park_sign.flavorText: Passing through parks will increase Duplicant Morale.

party_line_phone.name: Party Line Phone
party_line_phone.description: Can be used by one Duplicant to chat with themselves or other Duplicants in different locations. Chatting increases Duplicant Morale.
party_line_phone.summary: The Party Line Phone is a recreational building which gives a variable Morale boost based whether they are alone on the line or more when others are on the line:
party_line_phone.flavorText: You never know who you'll meet on the other line.

pedestal.name: Pedestal
pedestal.description: Displays a single object, doubling its Decor value.Objects with negative decor will gain some positive decor when displayed.
pedestal.summary: The Pedestal is a decorative Building that can be configured to display up to 1 kg or 1 unit of various resources. A Pedestal with nothing on it will grant +5 Decor with a radius of 1 tile. Pedestals with items on it gains additional Decor depending on the item being displayed:
pedestal.flavorText: Perception can be drastically changed by a bit of thoughtful presentation.

portrait_canvas.name: Portrait Canvas
portrait_canvas.description: Moderately increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be painted by a Duplicant.
portrait_canvas.summary: The Portrait Canvas is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be painted on to provide extra.
portrait_canvas.flavorText: Once built, a Duplicant can paint a blank canvas to produce a decorative painting.

sauna.name: Sauna
sauna.description: Uses Steam to create a relaxing atmosphere.Increases Duplicant Morale.
sauna.summary: The Sauna is used during downtime to improve a Duplicant's morale. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a recreational room. A Duplicant spends 30 seconds in the sauna.
sauna.flavorText: A steamy sauna soothes away all the aches and pains
sauna.requires: Preferred Room: Recreation RoomSteam: 25 kg per use

sculpting_block.name: Sculpting Block
sculpting_block.description: Majorly increases Decor, contributing to Morale.Must be sculpted by a Duplicant.
sculpting_block.summary: The Sculpting Block is a decorative building in the Furniture category that provides a Decor bonus to its surroundings and can be sculpted to provide a further bonus. The quality of the sculpture influences this bonus with higher quality sculptures from highly skilled artistic Duplicants producing the best Decor bonuses.
sculpting_block.flavorText: Duplicants who have learned art skills can produce more decorative sculptures.

soda_fountain.name: Soda Fountain
soda_fountain.description: Creates soda from Water and Carbon Dioxide.Consuming soda water increases Duplicant Morale.
soda_fountain.summary: The Soda Fountain is used during downtime and will apply the "Soda Filled" attribute raising a Duplicant's morale by 4 and Science attribute by 1. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a Recreational Room.
soda_fountain.flavorText: Sparkling water puts a twinking in a Duplicant's eye.
soda_fountain.requires: Prefered Room: Recreational RoomCarbon Dioxide: 1000g per useWater: 5kg per use

sun_lamp.name: Sun Lamp
sun_lamp.description: Gives off Sunlight level Lux.Can be paired with a Beach Chair.
sun_lamp.summary: The Sun Lamp is a recreational building that provides sunlight level Lux. It can be paired with the Beach Chair to improve the morale bonus from the Beach Chair.

vertical_wind_tunnel.name: Vertical Wind Tunnel
vertical_wind_tunnel.description: Must be placed above an open space and be connected to a Power Source.Increases Duplicants Morale.
vertical_wind_tunnel.summary: The Vertical Wind Tunnel is used during downtime to improve a Duplicant's morale and reduce stress. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a recreational room. At most 3 Duplicants can use the Vertical Wind Tunnel at once, and can enter/exit while running. Each use of the Vertical Wind Tunnel takes 90 seconds. Using the Vertical Wind Tunnel may cause Popped Eardrums in areas with poor circulation if gas pressure exceeds 4 kg.
vertical_wind_tunnel.flavorText: Duplicants love the feeling of high-powered wind through their hair.

wall_pot.name: Wall Pot
wall_pot.description: Houses a single Plant when sown with a Seed.Increases Decor, contributing to Morale.
wall_pot.summary: The Wall Pot can host a decorative plant which will increase Decor in the surrounding area. However, if the plant's needs are not met (and the plant becomes Stifled), it will lower decor instead.
wall_pot.flavorText: Must be hung from a wall.Placing a plant in a wall pot can add a spot of decor to otherwise bare walls.

water_cooler.name: Water Cooler
water_cooler.description: Provides a gathering place for Duplicants during Downtime.Improves Duplicant Morale.
water_cooler.summary: The Water Cooler is a recreational building. A single Duplicant can use Water Cooler during downtime to slightly improve morale. Despite being a recreational building, it does not need to be in a Recreational Room.
water_cooler.flavorText: Chatting with friends improves Duplicants' moods and reduces their stress.

medicine_building.name: Medicine (Building)
medicine_building.description: Medicine buildings focus on Duplicant's health.

apothecary.name: Apothecary
apothecary.description: Produces Medicine to cure most basic Diseases.Duplicants must possess the Medicine Compounding Skill to fabricate medicines.
apothecary.summary: TheApothecary is a Medicine building used to make medicinal items such as pills and items consumed by other medicine buildings.
